title: The Light Of Stars.
content:
The night is come, but not too soon;
        And sinking silently,
    All silently, the little moon
        Drops down behind the sky.

    T

title: Footsteps Of Angels.
content:

title: The Beleaguered City.
content:
I have read, in some old marvellous tale,
        Some legend strange and vague,
    That a midnight host of spectres pale

title: Woods In Winter.
content:
When winter winds are piercing chill
        And through the hawthorn blows the gale,
    With solemn feet I tread the hill,

title: An April Day.
content:
When the warm sun, that brings
    Seed-time and harvest, has returned again,
    'Tis sweet to visit the still wood, where springs

title: Voices Of The Night. Prelude.
content:

title: Midnight Mass For The Dying Year.
content:
Yes, the Year is growing old,

title: Hymn Of The Moravian Nuns Of Bethlehem, At The Consecration Of Pulaski's Banner.
content:
When the dying flame of day

        Far the glimmering tapers shed

title: The Terrestrial Paradise. From Dante, Purgatorio, XXVIII.
content:
Longing already to search in and round
    The heavenly forest, dense and living green,
    Which to the eyes tempered the new-born day,

title: Song Of The Bell.  From The German.
content:
Bell! thou soundest merrily,
    When the bridal party
        To the church doth hie!
    Bell! thou soundest solemnly,
    When, on Sabbath

title: Hymn To The Night.
content:
I heard the trailing garments of the Night
        Sweep through her marble halls!
    I saw her sable skirts all fringed with light
        Fr

title: King Christian. A National Song Of Denmark. From The Danish Of Johannes Evald.
content:
King Christian stood by the lofty mast
        In mist and smoke;
    His sword was hammering so fast,

title: Flowers.
content:
Spake full well, in language quaint and olden,
        One who dwelleth by the castled Rhine,
    When he called the flowers, so blue and golden

title: The Spirit Of Poetry.
The Celestial Pilot.  From Dante's Purgatorio, II.
content:
And now, behold! as at the approach of morning
    Through the gross vapours, Mars grows fiery red
    Down in the west upon the ocean floor.

title: Burial Of The Minnisink.
content:
On sunny slope and beechen swell
    The shadowed light of evening fell:
    And, where the maple's leaf was brown,

title: Sunrise On The Hills.
content:
I stood upon the hills, when heaven's wide arch
    Was glorious with the sun's returning march,
    And woods were brightened, and soft gales

title: Spring.
content:
From the French of Charles D'Orleans, Fifteenth Century.

    Gentle Spring! - in sunshine clad,
        Well dost thou thy power display!

title: Autumn.
content:
With what a glory comes and goes the year!
    The buds of spring, those beautiful harbingers
    Of sunny skies and cloudless times, enjoy

title: A Psalm Of Life.  What The Heart Of The Young Man Said To The Psalmist.
content:
Tell me not, in mournful numbers,
        "Life is but an empty dream!"
    For the soul is dead that slumbers.
        And things are not what

title: The Reaper And The Flowers.
content:
There is a Reaper, whose name is Death,
        And, with his sickle keen,
